producer: demiurge RTSC §8.5 — material-side -> device-side conductor handoff schema exemplar. HTS REBCO baseline, hexa-rtsc-derived (NOT vendor lot data). This is the schema STUB the Swift cockpit ConductorRecord/ConductorLoader will ingest (deferred, RTSC.md §8.6), not real characterization data.
stub_note: Schema exemplar only. spec.jc_b_t_theta and the scalar property values are PLACEHOLDER/null with documented units — a real conductor record lands either from a vendor datasheet ingest (gate_type=vendor-datasheet) or from a hexa-rtsc Tier-1 simulation export (this record's gate_type=hexa-rtsc-derived). Sibling vendor-datasheet exemplar: exports/conductor/rebco_hts_baseline.json.

spec.family_enum_note: ConductorMaterial = cu | nbti | nb3sn | rebco | bi2212 | rtsc_hypothetical (RTSC.md §5 Axis B / §8.6). This record = rebco. spec.family maps 1:1 to the ConductorMaterial enum case (RTSC.md §8.6 deferred item).
spec.compound: REBa2Cu3O7-delta (RE = Y, Gd, ...; '2G HTS' coated conductor)
spec.tc_k_typical_note: REBCO bulk Tc ~ 90-93 K at ambient pressure; left null in this stub — populated on ingest.

jc_b_t_theta.description: Critical current density as a function of applied field B, temperature T, and field-to-tape-plane angle theta. The device producer (getdp_hts.py) reads this to fill Jc_limit / Hc2_limit / nu[Coil]. Left as a stub here — a real Jc(B,T,theta) table lands as a separate Tier-3 measurement/datasheet record and is referenced by jc_data_table_ref_record_id.

B.description: magnitude of applied magnetic flux density

theta.description: angle of B relative to the tape ab-plane (0 deg = B parallel to tape face / ab-plane peak, 90 deg = B perpendicular / c-axis minimum)

value.jc_units: A/m^2 (engineering Je often reported in A/mm^2 or Ic in A/cm-width; converted on ingest)
value.model_note: Empirical fits (e.g. Kim-like or percolation/pinning models) populate this; E-J power-law index n=20-30 for REBCO (RTSC.md §2 Axis B). Null in this stub.

provenance.source_citation: hexa-rtsc derived baseline (closed-form / Tier-1 simulation lineage). Stub — no concrete derive record wired yet; the thin adapter that ingests hexa-rtsc numerics_*_solver.hexa results into exports/conductor/hexa-rtsc-baseline/ is deferred (RTSC.md §8.6 deferred list).
provenance.derive_chain: hexa-rtsc numerics_*_solver.hexa -> (deferred thin adapter, RTSC.md §8.6) -> this conductor record -> device producer (getdp_hts.py) reads nu[Coil]/Jc_limit/Hc2_limit

gate_type_enum_note: gate_type in { vendor-datasheet | hexa-rtsc-derived | external-paper } per RTSC.md §8.5. Claim-only / unproven families (rtsc_hypothetical) use gate_type=empirically-unproven (RTSC.md §8.6). This REBCO baseline is derived, not measured -> absorbed stays false.
scope_caveats: STUB record — schema exemplar, not real conductor characterization. All spec property values are null placeholders with documented units.

hexa-rtsc-derived means closed-form / simulation lineage, NOT a measurement and NOT a vendor lot. absorbed=false is therefore permanent for the derived values until an independent measurement record supersedes them (RTSC.md §8.7 Tier-3).

jc_b_t_theta is the load-bearing field the device producer needs (getdp_hts.py fills Jc_limit/Hc2_limit from it); it is a stub here. A real Jc(B,T,theta) table lands as a separate record referenced via jc_data_table_ref_record_id.
absorbed (device-side absorption) is tracked separately from this material-side handoff; flipping it requires the full non-wet-lab gate set, not this record alone.

Sibling vendor-path exemplar exists at exports/conductor/rebco_hts_baseline.json (gate_type=vendor-datasheet, vendor=SuperPower). This file is the complementary hexa-rtsc-derived / vendor=null exemplar requested by RTSC.md §8.6.
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · provisional=true · gate_type=hexa-rtsc-derived · domain=conductor. R4 preserved — a derived/stub baseline is not a measurement; device-side absorption is downstream.
citations: RTSC.md §8.6 (exports/conductor/ + HTS REBCO baseline handoff schema stub milestone)
RTSC.md §5 Axis B / §2 (ConductorMaterial enum: cu | nbti | nb3sn | rebco | bi2212 | rtsc_hypothetical; REBCO E-J power-law n=20-30, Jc(B,T,theta) anisotropy)
RTSC.md §8.7 (4-tier expansion path — Tier-1 computational / Tier-3 measurement-ingest)
